title: RecordedScenario
description: Manifest for a recorded replay scenario under examples/sftdd-scenarios/<name>/. A scenario is a self-contained corpus (recorded-artifacts/ design lane + recorded-build/ build corpus + turns/ per-turn timeline) plus this manifest, replayed by replay-scenario.sh and guarded by tests/bdd/sftdd-scenarios.test.ts. See examples/sftdd-scenarios/SCENARIOS.md.

name.description: Scenario slug; must match the scenario directory name.

description.description: One-line summary of what the scenario exercises.

uiTrack.description: Whether the scenario has a user-facing UI. The single source for the UX track: the capture harness passes it to create-project (--ui-track), which persists project.uiTrack (the drive reads it to run the UX Designer + design-guide/IA + adherence gate) and always wires the e2e harness for a UI project.

language.description: Project language stack to scaffold (create-project --language). Omit to use create-project's default.

runner.description: CI runner type to scaffold (create-project --runner). Omit to use create-project's default.

features.description: The features replayed in order (each from its recorded-artifacts/ design + recorded-build/ build corpus).

id.description: Feature id; must match a recorded-artifacts/features/<id>/ + recorded-build/features/<id>/ dir.

buildReplay.description: Whether a recorded build corpus exists to restore (skip navigator/driver). false = design-only replay, live build.

summary.description: Optional one-line note on what this feature demonstrates (e.g. additive vs contract/cleanup).

pauseBefore.description: Default handoff the live replay drives to (lakebase-sftdd-drive --pause-before).
